description_long: This is a large, military-style cargo airplane with a robust fuselage and a high-mounted straight wing configuration. The aircraft features four prominent turboprop engines, each with multi-blade propellers mounted on the wings. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted low on the tail. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage, with a slightly bulbous nose and multiple cockpit windows visible. The exterior is finished in a matte, dark olive or military green color, with minimal visible markings. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retractable or not modeled.
description: A large, military-style cargo airplane with four turboprop engines, high straight wings, and a conventional tail, finished in matte dark green.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide wingspan, four propellers, and the cockpit windows at the nose.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, high-mounted wings, and the profile of the tail and engines.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, engine placement, and the overall planform of the airplane.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the other side of the fuselage and wing-mounted engines.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable or not modeled.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the nose of the fuselage.
